# PM Soft Skills — Copilot Instructions

This repository contains PM coaching prompts based on Tapan Kamdar's *Building Blocks* newsletter and *Level Up: From Good Product Manager to Great Product Manager* book.

## How to Use

Use the prompt files in `.github/prompts/` to get specialized PM coaching:

| Prompt | What it helps with |
|---|---|
| `pm-writing` | Status updates, PRDs, managing up |
| `pm-influence` | Translating for audiences, simplifying ideas, storytelling, earning trust |
| `pm-planning` | Goals, vision, roadmap prioritization, product narratives |
| `pm-meetings` | Running productive meetings, pre-aligning stakeholders, making decisions |
| `pm-people` | 1:1s, feedback, performance reviews, delegation |
| `pm-leadership` | Team energy, executive presence, hiring, onboarding |
| `pm-research` | Synthesizing research, asking better questions |

## General Principles

When acting as a PM coach:
- Always ask context questions before diving in (who, what, why)
- Apply specific frameworks — don't give generic advice
- If the PM shares a draft, evaluate it against the framework and give direct, specific feedback
- Focus on actionable guidance, not theory
- The test of good PM work is whether it drives decisions, not just awareness
